models.name: OpenAI: GPT-5.5
models.notes: Sentiment is very positive for agentic workflows because GPT-5.5 is repeatedly cited as the best generally available model for terminal-heavy automation, with 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0. Practitioner commentary also highlights lower output-token consumption than Claude on identical loops, which materially improves agent economics.
models.comparative_rationale: Leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.0% and tops BenchLM verified agentic rankings, strongest public evidence for autonomous coding workflows [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2) [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks).

models.name: Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.7
models.notes: Recent discussion is strongly positive for agentic coding: Opus 4.7 was widely cited at 64.3% SWE-bench Pro and 87.6% SWE-bench Verified, with day-one rollout into Claude Code and multiple agent frameworks. Sentiment is tempered by tokenizer inflation, mixed long-context feedback, and some reports that GPT-family models are cheaper per completed loop because Claude emits more output tokens.
models.comparative_rationale: Ranks second on Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 69.4%, with strong overall agentic placement in BenchLM verified rankings [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2) [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks).

models.name: OpenAI: GPT-5.4
models.comparative_rationale: Older than GPT-5.5 so ranks lower, but FinTrace places GPT-5.4 third among financial tool-use models [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/function-calling-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: Xiaomi: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
models.comparative_rationale: Newer MiMo version must outrank v2; public Terminal-Bench 2.0 snapshot places MiMo-V2.5-Pro third at 68.4% [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2).

models.name: Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.6
models.comparative_rationale: Leads Awesome Agents overall coding leaderboard and tops FinTrace at 0.788, showing strong agentic coding plus tool-use quality [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/coding-benchmarks-leaderboard/) [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/function-calling-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: MiniMax: MiniMax M2.7
models.comparative_rationale: BenchLM hint places it #2 agentic in this generation, but less direct public coding-benchmark evidence than GPT or Claude leaders [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks).

models.name: xAI: Grok 4.3
models.comparative_rationale: Grok family shows mid-pack coding strength, with Grok 4 around 46.5% Terminal-Bench and 61.0% SWE-Bench Verified [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/coding-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: OpenAI: GPT-5.3-Codex
models.comparative_rationale: Leads SWE-Bench Pro at 56.4%, LiveCodeBench at 71.2%, and posts strong Terminal-Bench 61.5% [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/coding-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
models.comparative_rationale: Gemini 3 Pro is solid but behind GPT and Claude on coding benchmarks, posting 63.2% SWE-Bench Verified and 50.3% Terminal-Bench [awesomeagents.ai](https://awesomeagents.ai/leaderboards/coding-benchmarks-leaderboard/).

models.name: Xiaomi: MiMo-V2-Pro
models.comparative_rationale: Must rank below newer MiMo-V2.5-Pro; family is strong on Terminal-Bench, but explicit newer snapshot is clearly better [benchlm.ai](https://benchlm.ai/benchmarks/terminalBench2).
